class Solution:

solution: 
```python
import math
from typing import List

class Solution:
    def constructRectangle(self, area: int) -> List[int]:
        w = math.isqrt(area)
        while True:
            if area % w == 0:
                return [area // w, w]
            w -= 1
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(√n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution starts from the integer square root of the area and checks downward for the largest valid width divisor. This minimizes L-W difference efficiently. Strengths include optimal early termination and simplicity. Weaknesses are potential linear checks in worst-case scenarios, though constrained by √n limit.
